description_long: This object is a compact handheld two-way radio, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, featuring a long, flexible antenna extending from the top. The front face displays a small rectangular screen, likely for channel or status information, and several colored buttons for operation. The body is primarily black with a textured grip on the sides for secure handling, and a prominent rotary knob and button on the top for power and volume control. The side view reveals a push-to-talk button and additional controls, typical for communication devices. The back is plain, likely housing the battery compartment. This device is designed for portable communication in urban environments, such as for security personnel or event staff.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, screen, colored buttons, and textured grip. Designed for portable two-way communication.
description_view_0: Side view showing the textured grip and a prominent push-to-talk button.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the screen, colored buttons, speaker grille, and antenna.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with additional grip texture and possible secondary controls.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain back, likely the battery compartment, and the top-mounted rotary knob.
